name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans (up to $1,000), and title loans (up to $25,000) with same-day funding at physical locations and online.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized lender operating physical storefronts across the U.S., including this Jacksonville, Florida location at 1020-14 N. Edgewood Ave. The company has been serving customers seeking quick access to emergency cash for decades and maintains a 4.9-star rating across 125,448 customer reviews on Google. Their business model centers on short-term lending solutions designed to bridge financial gaps between paychecks or cover unexpected expenses.

Advance America offers three primary loan products: Payday Loans (2–4 week terms, $100–$500), Installment Loans (larger amounts up to $1,000 with flexible repayment schedules), and Title Loans (up to $25,000 using vehicle equity as collateral, offered in partnership with LoanCenter). All products are available both online and in-store. The Jacksonville location operates extended hours (10 a.m.–6 p.m. weekdays, 9 a.m.–6 p.m. Friday, 9 a.m.–4 p.m. Saturday) and provides bilingual customer service ("Hablamos español"). The application process requires government ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN.

Advance America differentiates itself through same-day loan approval and funding, a large branch network enabling in-person service, high customer satisfaction ratings, and flexible loan amounts across three tiers. The company also offers Western Union services at this location. Their staff is trained to explain loan terms and help customers select appropriate products for their situations. The physical location in Commonwealth Village provides accessibility for Jacksonville residents who prefer face-to-face interactions.

While Advance America provides genuine convenience for emergency borrowing, the website does not disclose APR, fees, or interest rates—critical information for evaluating cost. Payday and installment loans typically carry substantially higher annual percentage rates than traditional bank loans, sometimes exceeding 400% APR depending on state law. Title loans require collateral (a paid-off vehicle) and carry repossession risk. These products are genuinely emergency solutions rather than long-term financial tools, and the lack of transparent pricing on the website is a notable limitation for consumers evaluating affordability.
pros: Same-day funding available for payday and installment loans up to $1,000
Flexible loan amounts: $100–$500 payday loans, $100–$1,000 installment loans, $2,000–$25,000 title loans
Multiple application methods: online, in-store, or by phone pre-qualification
Extended store hours (10 a.m.–6 p.m. weekdays, 9 a.m.–4 p.m. Saturday) for working customers
High customer satisfaction: 4.9-star rating across 125,448 Google reviews
Installment loans offer longer repayment terms than payday loans, reducing immediate payment burden
cons: Website does not disclose APR, fees, or interest rates—consumers must visit in-store or call for pricing
Payday and installment loans typically carry very high annual percentage rates (often 300–500% APR) compared to traditional lending
Title loans require collateral (paid-off vehicle in borrower's name) and carry repossession risk if not repaid
Short repayment terms on payday loans (2–4 weeks) can create debt cycles if income is unstable
No mention of credit counseling, debt management alternatives, or financial education resources on the website
best_for: Consumers with sudden emergencies (car repairs, medical bills, utility shutoffs) requiring cash within 24 hours
Unbanked or underbanked customers without access to credit cards or traditional bank loans
Vehicle owners with paid-off cars who need larger amounts ($2,000–$25,000) via title loans
Customers who prefer in-person service and bilingual support for loan application
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers in genuine emergencies who need cash within 24 hours and have limited access to traditional credit. However, the primary caveat is that payday and installment loans carry very high costs (often 300–500%+ APR) and short repayment periods that can create debt cycles; these are emergency solutions only, not sustainable financial products. Consumers should explore payday alternatives (credit union PALs, employer advances, non-profit credit counseling) before committing to these high-cost loans.
services: Payday loans ($100–$500, 2–4 week terms, in-store and online)
Installment loans ($100–$1,000, multiple payment terms, in-store and online)
Title loans ($2,000–$25,000, vehicle collateral required, in partnership with LoanCenter)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
